items.title: Closures & first-class functions
items.description: A decorator's wrapper is a closure that captures the original function; this only works because functions are first-class values that can be passed and returned.

items.title: functools.wraps
items.description: wraps is itself a decorator that copies __name__, __doc__ and other metadata from the original function onto the wrapper.

items.title: Context managers
items.description: Both add cross-cutting behavior, but decorators wrap a whole function while context managers (with-blocks) wrap a region of code at the call site.
